_comment_target: Action-conditioned legacy variant v2 (ActionConditionedTembAdapter) on the full 802-clip droid_1k train split, FSDP. v2 = render Q ← cross_attn → 8-d (joint+gripper) actions K/V → LayerNorm → out_proj (kaiming-init, NOT zero) → ADD to temb_base → time_proj. NO zero-init / NO gates: mirrors egowm init philosophy. Earlier zero-init out_proj caused a rank-1 collapse (cross_attn.in_proj_weight welded at xavier, contribution carrying only a constant dataset-mean offset — see memory:project_v2_adapter_collapse). 17 frames @ 240×432 for a fast A/B against the egowm 30-epoch baseline (run_20260507_052841) at matched resolution + length.
_metadata_note: Full 802-row train split — matches the egowm baseline's metadata exactly. 17-frame loader needs only ≥18 mp4 frames, so the 81-frame filtering is unnecessary here.
_num_frames_note: 17 = matches egowm baseline. T_lat=5 post-patch frames × 30×54 spatial = 8100 tokens. ~4× faster per step than 81 frames; loss/PSNR comparable to egowm in this regime so the action-conditioning contribution is the variable we're measuring.
_cache_note: v2 17-frame cache. Cache key includes has_actions=True + action_field=state, so it is distinct from the egowm 17-frame cache at precompute_cache_17f.pt — they will NOT collide. First launch MISSES; subsequent launches HIT.
_val_note: Held-out 10-scene set at data_wan_eval/eval_metadata.csv (scenes 10000-10009 — never seen during training on data_wan_1k). Runs every 2 epochs at 4 fixed timesteps per sample, deterministic noise → stable val curve.
